channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Draft budget plan proposes deep cuts across federal health programs
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A draft budget proposal circulating among federal officials would dramatically deepen cuts at the nation's top health agency, eliminating some public health programs entirely and serving as a roadmap for more mass firings. The document suggests a cut in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal judge will wait to rule on an effort to block Trump's executive order on elections
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday said she would wait to decide whether to grant a preliminary injunction to national Democrats and voting rights groups that are challenging President Donald Trump’s recent executive order on elections. U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nearly 90% of Consumer Financial Protection Bureau cut as as Trump's government downsizing continues
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is drastically shrinking the size and the mission of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, the latest step in an extraordinary reshaping of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Politics Friday: Digging into Minnesota‘s political history
item.description: Friday at noon, MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst and a team of MPR News reporters and producers share a bit Minnesota political history with some modern twists.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Afghanistan Medal of Honor recipient reenlists in Marine Corps reserves after 15 years
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Dakota Meyer, a Marine who was awarded the Medal of Honor for heroism in the Afghanistan War but later became a sharp critic of the Biden administration over its chaotic withdrawal from that conflict, has reenlisted in the military and will serve in the Marine Reserves.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: DNC official sparks Dem civil war
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Appeals court denies DOJ bid to block return of Kilmar Abrego Garcia from El Salvador prison
item.description: A federal court rejected a DOJ bid to block the return of Maryland illegal immigrant Kilmar Abrego Garcia from El Salvador, warning the Trump administration must not override due process or rule of law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Protesters gather outside El Salvador’s consulate in St. Paul
item.description: Protesters gathered outside El Salvador’s consulate in St. Paul on Wednesday to demand the return of a man who was mistakenly sent to a prison in that Central American nation.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff and The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court to hear oral arguments in birthright citizenship case
item.description: The Supreme Court is set to hear arguments in May over President Donald Trump’s executive order aimed at ending birthright citizenship for children of noncitizens.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gabbard declassifies Biden counterterrorism strategy, confirms push for information-sharing with Big Tech
item.description: A newly declassified Biden-era strategy to counter domestic terrorism called for information sharing with Big Tech, banning assault weapons and curbing ghost guns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Some Pennsylvania Jews reminded of Tree of Life shooting after arson attack on Josh Shapiro's home
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A year ago, Carole Zawatsky was invited by Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro to a Passover Seder, held in the same room at the governor’s official residence that was devastated by an arsonist just hours after Shapiro hosted this year’s Jewish holiday feast.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, MARK SCOLFORO and JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court to hear rare May arguments in birthright citizenship battle
item.description: The Supreme Court scheduled a rare May oral argument on the Trump administration’s emergency request to narrow a series of nationwide blocks on President Trump’s executive order that would restrict birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump admin guts AmeriCorps, Clinton-era volunteer agency that failed 8 consecutive audits
item.description: The Trump administration placed roughly 75% of full-time AmeriCorps employees on administrative leave Wednesday, Fox News Digital learned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republican uses George Washington, American Revolution to reject notion Ukraine should surrender to Russia
item.description: Rep. Don Bacon pointed to the American Revolution to push back against the idea that Ukraine should surrender to Russia. Bacon has advocated for the U.S. and Europe to "arm Ukraine to the max.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration plans to end the IRS Direct File program for free tax filing, AP sources say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration plans to eliminate the IRS' Direct File program, an electronic system for filing tax returns directly to the agency for free, according to two people familiar with the decision.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats’ vice chair ignites civil war, targeting 'asleep at the wheel' incumbents in primaries
item.description: Democratic Party vice chair David Hogg's financing primary challenges against establishment Democrats is exposing intra-party tensions rocking the party in its Trump-term wilderness.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Noem announces cancellation of $2.7 million in grants to Harvard
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem on Wednesday announced the cancellation of more than $2.7 million in grants to Harvard University amid tensions between the school and the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge says labor unions' lawsuit over DOGE access to Labor Department systems can move forward
item.description: A federal judge says he won't dismiss a lawsuit from labor unions seeking to block Elon Musk's team from accessing systems at the Labor Department.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House invites Rachel Morin's mother to press briefing to share daughter's story
item.description: The mother of a Maryland woman who was killed by an individual who entered the country illegally addressed reporters at the White House on Wednesday as the Trump administration seeks to go on offense over the case of an individual who was mistakenly deported to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin squeaks past Minnesota for the top spot in voter turnout
item.description: The Minnesota Secretary of State confirms Wisconsin narrowly surpassed Minnesota with voter turnout in the 2024 election.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters and Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: Kilmar Abrego Garcia's wife sought restraining order in 2021, DHS says
item.description: The wife of Kilmar Abrego Garcia filed for an order of protection against her husband in 2021, saying he was violent toward her.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: PIX on Politics Daily: NYC mayoral election poll
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Welcome to PIX on Politics Daily with Dan Mannarino, where we break down the day's political news, headlines, and issues that matter most to you through in-depth conversation. Join us daily on PIX11+ streaming at 1 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Judge: 'Probable cause' to hold U.S. in contempt over Alien Enemies Act deportations
item.description: The government sent several planeloads of alleged gang members to El Salvador, including 137 people under the act, the White House said at the time. The judge gave the U.S. until April 23 to respond.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff, MPR News

item.title: Funding for NYC Pre-K and 3-K permanently locked in: Mayor
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) -- For the first time ever, New York City has permanently locked in funding for its Pre-K and 3-K programming, Mayor Eric Adams announced on Wednesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, PIX 11

item.title: Retail sales jump as people rush to buy cars ahead of tariffs
item.description: The key driver of the economy saw the biggest increase in over two years in March, as car buyers tried to get ahead of President Donald Trump's tariffs on imported autos and auto parts.
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh, MPR News

item.title: Keri Heintzeman wins Republican primary in race to fill Justin Eichorn’s state Senate seat
item.description: The winner of the April 29 special election will fill the District 6 seat left vacant by Justin Eichorn, who resigned last month after he was charged with soliciting a minor.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: NIL payments OK at UNLV, UNR, but payment amounts secret under SB293
item.description: A bill concerning NIL — name, image, likeness — and how UNLV and UNR can manage contracts directly with players passed on Tuesday in the Nevada Senate, advancing to the Assembly with other bills. A Republican leader objected to secrecy surrounding the payment amounts.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gov. Shapiro arson attack: Religious bias suspected, DA says
item.description: "We do believe that there was a religious bias involved, and we're going to pursue that in our prosecution," Chardo said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Marshall man claims ICE detained him for protests over police killings
item.description: A Marshall man was detained by federal immigration authorities who claim he overstayed his student visa after it was revoked. However, he tells MPR News from the Kandiyohi County Jail that his arrest may have been politically motivated.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: A whistleblower’s disclosure details how DOGE may have taken sensitive labor data
item.description: A whistleblower tells Congress and NPR that DOGE may have taken sensitive labor data and hid its tracks. “None of that information should ever leave the agency,” said a former NLRB official.
item.dc:creator: Jenna McLaughlin, MPR News

item.title: In Pelican Rapids, teens learn the skill of civil discourse
item.description: In a deeply polarized country, a new program in Minnesota is teaching middle and high schoolers how to find common ground in their communities. Program leaders say it’s an effort to inspire young people to become engaged politically and in their towns.
item.dc:creator: Catharine Richert, MPR News

item.title: Pritzker says he's undecided about testifying before Congressional panel
item.description: The US House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform requested testimony from Pritzker, other Democrats about sanctuary laws.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9

item.title: Cuomo housing plan faces scrutiny
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Candidates in the race for New York City mayor are calling out former Gov. Andrew Cuomo for his housing plan. They claim it is light on details and that part of it was written using ChatGPT.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Minneapolis council member Emily Koski drops out of mayor's race
item.description: Minneapolis City Council member Emily Koski has dropped out of the mayor’s race, according to a campaign update Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News
